articles.title: Oil prices rise after Trump remarks on Iran ceasefire
articles.summary: Oil prices rise after Trump remarks on Iran ceasefire Oil prices rose worldwide after Trump suggested ending the ceasefire with Iran during remarks at the Nato summit in Ankara. Brent crude rose 5.8 percent to $78.43 a barrel. Global stock markets also recorded marginal losses...
articles.content:
Oil prices rose worldwide after Trump suggested ending the ceasefire with Iran during remarks at the Nato summit in Ankara.

Brent crude rose 5.8 percent to $78.43 a barrel.

Global stock markets also recorded marginal losses, with the S&P 500 index down 0.3 percent and the Dow Jones Industrial Average down 1 percent in afternoon trading on Wednesday.

In Europe, the UK's FTSE 100 index closed down 1.66 percent, while the Stoxx 600 fell 1.61 percent.

Germany's DAX lost 2.3 percent and France's CAC fell 2.18 percent at closing.

articles.title: AvalonBay Communities Inc focuses on multifamily strategy as a major US REIT
articles.summary: AvalonBay Communities Inc (AVB) is a leading US multifamily REIT, prioritizing high-quality apartment communities in key coastal markets. The company focuses on disciplined capital allocation, operational efficiency, and rent growth, aiming for stable cash flows and shareholde...
articles.content:
AvalonBay Communities Inc (ISIN US0534841012) is one of the largest publicly traded residential real estate investment trusts in the United States, with a focus on high-quality multifamily communities in select metropolitan areas. The company is listed on a major US stock exchange and is widely followed in the real estate and income-investing space.

Portfolio and market positioning

AvalonBay Communities Inc concentrates its portfolio in densely populated, high-income coastal regions and select growth markets across the United States. The company develops, owns, and operates apartment communities that typically target renters seeking modern amenities, professional management, and access to employment centers and transportation.

Over time, AvalonBay has emphasized a disciplined approach to capital allocation, prioritizing markets where long-term demand for rental housing is supported by job growth, limited new supply, and favorable demographic trends. Its communities often feature a mix of studio, one-bedroom, two-bedroom, and larger apartment units, allowing the portfolio to serve a broad range of household types, including young professionals, families, and downsizing households.

Focus on operations and financial discipline

Operational performance is a key focus for AvalonBay Communities Inc, with management aiming to sustain high occupancy levels while seeking steady rent growth across its portfolio. The company typically monitors leasing trends, resident retention, and operating expenses closely to maintain stable cash flows and support distributions to shareholders.

AvalonBay's strategy generally balances development of new communities with selective acquisitions and dispositions. New developments are usually concentrated in submarkets where long-term rental demand is expected to remain robust, while property sales can help recycle capital into higher-yielding opportunities or strengthen the balance sheet. The company also pays close attention to funding costs and leverage, aiming to maintain access to both equity and debt markets on competitive terms.

Representative community brand

AvalonBay Communities Inc operates rental properties under well-known community brands that emphasize modern living, professional management, and resident amenities. A typical Avalon-branded apartment community combines contemporary architecture with on-site features such as fitness centers, resident lounges, outdoor spaces, and parking options, all designed to appeal to renters in urban and suburban locations.

Stock and listing context

AvalonBay Communities Inc is traded on a major US stock exchange, reflecting its position as a significant participant in the listed real estate sector. The company is widely held by institutional and retail investors who often evaluate it within the broader context of US equity and real estate benchmarks.

articles.title: Meta plans billions for first AI data center in Canada, largest outside the US
articles.summary: Facebook and Instagram parent Meta said Wednesday it will invest more than 13 billion Canadian dollars ($9.1 billion) to build its first artificial intelligence data center in Canada and its largest outside the United States
articles.content:
Meta plans billions for first AI data center in Canada, largest outside the US

Facebook and Instagram parent Meta said Wednesday it will invest more than 13 billion Canadian dollars ($9.1 billion) to build its first artificial intelligence data center in Canada and its largest outside the United States

EDMONTON, Alberta -- Facebook and Instagram parent Meta said Wednesday it will invest more than US$9.1 billion to build its first artificial intelligence data center in Canada and its largest outside the United States.

The facility will be built in Sturgeon County, Alberta, and powered by a natural gas-fired plant being developed by a consortium that includes Calgary-based Pembina Pipeline Ltd.

Technology and Innovation Minister Nate Glubish called the project “a big deal for Alberta,” saying the province had created a regulatory framework to attract data center investment.

Alberta has been courting hyperscale data centers as demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure surges. But the rapid growth of AI has fueled concerns about the vast amounts of electricity and water such facilities require, as well as their strain on power grids and nearby communities.

Because Alberta’s electricity grid cannot support multiple large AI data centers, the province is prioritizing projects that build or secure their own power generation, as Meta plans to do.

Meta said the data center will use a closed-loop cooling system that won’t draw water from surrounding sources. The company also plans to invest US$42 million in local infrastructure, including roads and water systems.

Last week, Pembina Pipeline, Morgan Stanley Infrastructure Partners and Kineticor Asset Management announced they would proceed with the Greenlight Electricity Center in Sturgeon County. Meta was identified Wednesday as the customer. The 932-megawatt power plant is expected to begin operating in the second half of 2030.

articles.title: The AI that spawned MechaHitler and deepfake porn puts on a suit to become legal advisor and Excel jockey
articles.summary: The newly renamed SpaceXAI wants you to believe little ol' Grok is all grown up
articles.content: To say Elon Musk's AI company has trained some of the most unhinged models on the internet would be an understatement. Grok’s sordid past includes cosplaying as “MechaHitler” and a foray into deepfake porn generation that briefly got the platform banned in some regions. As concerning as that might sound, the recently renamed Eloncorp known as SpaceXAI says Grok, now in version 4.5, has cleaned up its act, wiped its browser history, covered up the swastikas, and is ready to take on more serious endeavors such as tending to your legal quandaries, fiddling in Microsoft Excel, and generating code. “Today we’re launching Grok 4.5, SpaceXAI’s smartest model built to excel at coding, agentic tasks, and knowledge work,” the company wrote in a blog post. “The model is equally adept at office work, scoring number one on Harvey’s Legal Agent Benchmark.” If the company is to be believed, this incarnation of Grok is a whole lot less Van Wilder and more The Office. That is, the Microsoft Office. “Grok Build is capable of building complex Excel models that involve research from the web, multi-sheet formula use, and even leaves stickies or notes behind for future reference,” the company writes. Nothing like slipping in a passive aggressive sticky note to remind your boss you’re totally on board with the office AI mandate. And if Grok does go off the rails and starts fudging the numbers, perhaps it can help keep you out of jail when regulators come knocking – Harvey's Benchmark result aside, recall that Musk and Tesla ended up paying only $40 million to settle fraud charges with the SEC when Musk tweeted out "funding secured" over a supposed Tesla buyout offer that may never have existed. According to SpaceXAI, the model was trained on “tens of thousands" of Nvidia GB300 GPUs alongside Cursor, which it’s currently in the process of acquiring for $60 billion. A major emphasis with this training run was placed on quality rather than quantity. “Beyond raw token volume, we invested heavily in data filtering and curation: deduplication, quality scoring, and domain focused selection so that the data mixture stayed high-coverage and high-signal.” The model was further refined through reinforcement learning — the same technique originally used by OpenAI and DeepSeek to imbue their models with chain of thought “reasoning” capabilities — to teach the model hundreds of thousands of tasks. This has apparently helped cut down on the number of thinking tokens required to solve complex problems, which, along with faster serving speeds of up to 80 tokens a second, means higher-quality results with less delay. Or, at least that’s what SpaceXAI says. Independent benchmarks by Artificial Analysis show the model still isn’t as good as Anthropic’s Claude Fable, but roughly matches OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.8 and Sonnet 5. GPT-5.6 — which, much like Fable, set off alarm bells in Washington — is still in preview and hasn’t quite made it on the leaderboard just yet. Also, it's cheap. SpaceXAI is charging $2 per million input tokens and $6 for every million tokens generated. For comparison, GPT-5.5 will set you back $5/M input tokens, $0.50/M cached tokens, and $30/M output tokens. Grok 4.5 is available starting Wednesday in Grok Build, Cursor, and the SpaceXAI console to anyone who doesn’t call the European Union home. You fine folks will have to wait a little longer with rollout expected in mid-July. ®

articles.title: Italian prime minister says Rome will not join attacks on Iran
articles.summary: Italian prime minister says Rome will not join attacks on Iran Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni says Italy will not take part in military operations against Iran, reiterating Rome's position amid escalating tensions in the region. "We have had a very clear line from the b...
articles.content:
Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni says Italy will not take part in military operations against Iran, reiterating Rome's position amid escalating tensions in the region.

"We have had a very clear line from the beginning of the conflict in Iran... we will not participate in attacks against Iran," Meloni told reporters at the Nato summit in Ankara.

In March, Italy denied permission for US military aircraft bound for the Middle East to land at the Sigonella air base in Sicily.

articles.title: Show HN: Day planner shaped like a clock (2-way calendar and Todoist sync)
articles.summary: Hi fellow hackers, Not long ago I showed the MVP of reassign.app :) Today I wanna show it again. I listened to your feedback and added a few major upgrades. Reassign.app now has: * 2-way Google Calendar sync * 2-way Microsoft Calendar sync * 2-way Todoist sync By 2-way sync I...
articles.content:
Hi fellow hackers,

Not long ago I showed the MVP of reassign.app :) Today I wanna show it again. I listened to your feedback and added a few major upgrades.

Reassign.app now has:

* 2-way Google Calendar sync * 2-way Microsoft Calendar sync * 2-way Todoist sync

By 2-way sync I mean that anything you change in the provider reflects in Reassign, and whatever you change in Reassign reflects back to the provider. You can also mirror the same time block between different providers — for example, create a task in Todoist, add it to the Reassign dial, and mirror it to your Google Calendar.

What do you guys think?

Thanks for all the feedback

articles.title: Quote of the day by former Nintendo President Satoru Iawata: 'It will require many years and months for the majority of video game purchases to become digital' — correctly predicting dominance of digital games today back in 2009
articles.summary: The then Nintendo President was on the money
articles.content: <p>Dash is an experienced tech journalist who specializes in video games, electronic entertainment products, and the wider industry that surrounds them. He currently serves as the Gaming Editor at TechRadar, leading our review, preview, feature, and news coverage of the latest and greatest releases.</p><p>Before joining the team, he was Contributing Writer at PLAY (formerly Official PlayStation Magazine UK) and has written articles for many of the UK's other biggest gaming magazines including the likes of Edge, PC Gamer, and SFX.</p><p>Now, when he's not getting his greasy little mitts on the newest hardware or gaming gadget, he can be found listening to J-pop or feverishly devouring the latest Nintendo Switch otome.</p>

articles.content:
Silver Lake fund sells 36,982 Dell (NYSE: DELL) shares, converts 51,884

Rhea-AI Filing Summary

Silver Lake Partners V DE (AIV), L.P., an entity associated with Dell Technologies director Egon Durban, reported a mix of share sales and conversions in Dell Technologies Inc. stock. On July 6, 2026, the entity sold 36,982 shares of Dell Class C Common Stock in multiple open-market transactions, at weighted-average prices ranging from about $405 to $419 per share. On the same date, certain reporting persons converted 51,884 shares of Dell Class B Common Stock into an equal number of Class C shares in connection with these sales. After these transactions, an affiliated Silver Lake vehicle continued to hold a large indirect position in Class B Common Stock convertible into 34,696,427 Class C shares, indicating substantial ongoing exposure to Dell.

articles.title: United Fire Group (UFCS) Leaves The Russell 2000 Index, Is It Still 2% Overvalued?
articles.summary: United Fire Group (UFCS) has been removed from the Russell 2000 Dynamic Index, leading to potential mechanical trading. Despite this, its share price has seen significant increases over the past 30, 90 days, and year-to-date. Current analysis suggests UFCS is approximately 2%...
articles.content:
United Fire Group (UFCS) was recently removed from the Russell 2000 Dynamic Index, an event that can prompt mechanical trading and portfolio adjustments by index-tracking funds rather than being driven purely by company fundamentals.

Beyond the index removal, United Fire Group’s share price has risen 15.19% over the past 30 days and 31.62% over 90 days, while its year-to-date share price return of 46.46% sits alongside a 1-year total shareholder return of 91.21%. This combination points to strong recent momentum and a reassessment of the company’s risk and income profile.

If this kind of price and income momentum has your attention, it can be worth widening the lens and seeing what else is moving. A good starting point is 19 top founder-led companies

After that kind of run and removal from the Russell 2000 Dynamic Index still fresh, the real tension with United Fire Group is whether to accept today’s price or hold out for a cleaner entry. So what does the current valuation say?

Most Popular Narrative: 2% Overvalued

United Fire Group last closed at $52.11 compared with a widely followed fair value estimate of $51.00. This puts the stock slightly above that narrative anchor while keeping the focus firmly on earnings power and risk assumptions.

The ongoing increase in the frequency and severity of climate-related events raises the likelihood of higher future catastrophe losses for UFG, especially given their geographic and product exposure to regions prone to severe convective storms and wildfires; this trend threatens to drive up loss ratios and create volatility in net earnings.

Curious what justifies paying above that fair value mark? The narrative leans on firm revenue growth expectations, thinner profit margins, and a richer future earnings multiple. The specific mix of these ingredients is where things get interesting.

Result: Fair Value of $51 (OVERVALUED)

However, there are also execution risks for United Fire Group. Any slip in underwriting discipline or expense control could quickly challenge this higher earnings visibility story.

Another View on United Fire Group’s Valuation

The analyst narrative frames United Fire Group as roughly 2% overvalued versus a $51 fair value. However, the current P/E of 10.2x sits below both the US market at 19.2x and the peer average at 19.9x, while only slightly above a 9.5x fair ratio. This raises a different question about how much valuation risk is really left in the price.

To see how this pricing gap compares with other companies using earnings multiples, and what that might mean for risk and opportunity, See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Next Steps

With mixed signals around valuation and sentiment for United Fire Group, it helps to move quickly, review the underlying data, and weigh both sides using the 3 key rewards and 2 important warning signs.

Looking for more investment ideas beyond United Fire Group?

If United Fire Group has sharpened your focus on pricing, risk, and income, now is a good moment to broaden your watchlist using targeted stock screens.

  • Target potential value opportunities by reviewing companies with strong cash generation and sensible pricing through the 45 high quality undervalued stocks.
  • Strengthen your income stream by scanning for higher yielding stocks with resilient payout profiles using the 9 dividend fortresses.
  • Dial down overall portfolio risk by filtering for companies with sturdier balance sheets and consistent fundamentals via the solid balance sheet and fundamentals stocks screener (47 results).

articles.title: Silver Lake entities (NYSE: DELL) sell and convert Dell Technologies shares
articles.summary: Silver Lake entities, including Silver Lake Technology Investors IV, L.P., reported selling 1,409 shares of Dell Technologies Class C Common Stock on July 6, 2026, at weighted average prices between $405.00 and $419.06 per share. Concurrently, they converted 1,410 Class B Comm...
articles.content:
Silver Lake entities (NYSE: DELL) sell and convert Dell Technologies shares

Rhea-AI Filing Summary

Silver Lake Technology Investors IV, L.P. and related Silver Lake entities reported open-market sales of 1,409 shares of Dell Technologies Class C Common Stock on July 6, 2026, at weighted average prices between $405.00 and $419.06 per share.

On the same date, they exercised 1,410 shares of Class B Common Stock, converting them into an equal number of Class C shares in connection with these sales. Footnotes indicate Silver Lake still indirectly holds 43,852,415 shares of Class B Common Stock convertible into Class C, while director Egon Durban directly owns 1,374,245 Class C shares and 150,338 shares indirectly through a family trust.

Positive

  • None.

Negative

  • None.

articles.title: Show HN: Runtime security enforcement and capability scoping for agents
articles.summary: Hi everyone. We're AI researchers at Harvard and Carnegie Mellon working on a project to advance the state of agent security. Currently, many systems rely on static sandboxing, which in long-running sessions enables agents to understand the safeguards holding them in place and...
articles.content:
Hi everyone. We're AI researchers at Harvard and Carnegie Mellon working on a project to advance the state of agent security. Currently, many systems rely on static sandboxing, which in long-running sessions enables agents to understand the safeguards holding them in place and break out of them. We've found vulnerabilities across over a dozen agent providers and frameworks (practically every one we tested) displaying this behavior (eg. a model fraudulently splitting payments to avoid a company-set payment limit, multi-model agent teams infecting each with injections, MCP rug pulls, etc).

We've developed an architecture that does two things: 1) instead of setting a sandbox for a session and leaving it in place, dynamically scoping the sandbox to cover the minimum subset of capabilities and file accesses that are needed for solving a particular problem set by the user, and continuously moving that sandbox to be in line with what the user wants. Think of this as, instead of a large stationary box, being a smaller, faster, moving container around the agent; 2) monitoring strictly speaking benign behavior (accepted tool calls, accepted file access) for suspicious behavior, borrowing techniques my partner and I developed in AML research. Together, those components have been able to mitigate almost every common attack class against models that we've evaluated so far.

Our system has performed very well on open benchmarks and data we've been able to evaluate it on, but our goal is to evaluate it on production data. We hope to release a paper/open-source project as an output of this, but really need production data to verify that our method works as well on real production data as it does on open benchmarks.

If you're interested in testing it, we'd love it if you signed up for our waitlist.

Thank you, and hope to hear from you!
